page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel CPU cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 64 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The underlying Intel processor includes an L3 cache of 260 MB, alongside 2 MB of L2 cache and 48 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) such as amx_tile and amx_int8, and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). Cryptographic acceleration is supported via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI flags, alongside standard integer and bit manipulation instructions like BMI2 and FMA.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance delivers mid-range performance across most categories. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1855.0 and a multi-core score of 11352.0. Text processing performance is a notable strength, scoring 2423.0, which places it in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only cached small block speed of 1107.55 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds reach 117.6 GB/sec and 67.32 GB/sec, respectively.

For specialized tasks, the instance processes LLM inference prompts at 1152.75 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, with text generation reaching a strong 282.68 tokens/sec. Static web serving throughput is measured at 22.14 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations reach 17.13 million operations per second. Given its balanced resource allocation and dedicated CPU execution, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, text processing, database operations, and moderate software compilation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-16 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores) and 64 GB of RAM. It features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio, a 260 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results indicate solid mid-range performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1855.0 and a multi-core score of 11352.0. It demonstrates strong capabilities in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, where it performs in the top 25%. With a static web serving throughput of 22.14 GB/sec and Redis SET performance of 17.13 million operations per second, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, database operations, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-16 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for balanced workload performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-16 is a general-purpose x86_64 instance with 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors, advanced matrix extensions, and a classic one-to-four vCPU-to-memory ratio.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM on x86_64 Intel architecture
Advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Strong performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations
